page: Microsoft Azure DC48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCasv6 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features an x86_64 AMD processor with 48 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs). It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disks. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance, and it does not feature any graphics processing units (GPUs).

From a resource density perspective, the DC48as_v6 offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without the overhead of specialized GPU hardware. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a viable option for general-purpose workloads, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded processing tasks that do not require local NVMe SSDs.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DCasv6 family, powered by an x86_64 AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to external high-performance storage volumes. With zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses and no onboard GPU, the DC48as_v6 is designed for workloads requiring stable, dedicated CPU resources. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention. This virtual machine provides a balanced resource profile for organizations deploying standard business applications, web serving, and medium-scale database hosting utilizing network-attached storage.
